pilot_assets.why_relevant: Primary behavioral and structural hotspot. Changes here alter the main execution, tool-routing, plugin-policy, agent-lifecycle, memory, and package seam assumptions that any repo analysis must start from.
analysis_implications: Treat core as the primary package ownership and blast-radius anchor.
Bias summaries toward agent lifecycle, tool result shape, plugin gating, and memory/realtime changes.
Expect many downstream surfaces to be explained by core changes rather than isolated crate-local behavior.
recent_signals: fix: case where agent is already closed (#15163)
feat: add graph representation of agent network (#15056)
feat: support product-scoped plugins. (#15041)
Add thread/shellCommand to app server API surface (#14988)
[hooks] turn_id extension for Stop & UserPromptSubmit (#15118)

pilot_assets.why_relevant: Directly shapes the request/response boundary for remote and app-server-backed analysis. Recent changes moved plugin/product scope, thread shell commands, realtime handling, and websocket behavior.
analysis_implications: Treat app-server as a first-class operational surface, not a thin transport detail.
When analyzing remote or TUI-driven workflows, inspect app-server before assuming behavior is owned by the UI.
Use it as a bounded high-signal package for package-aware summaries.
recent_signals: feat: support product-scoped plugins. (#15041)
Add thread/shellCommand to app server API surface (#14988)
Simple directory mentions (#14970)
Support featured plugins (#15042)
fix: harden plugin feature gating (#15104)

pilot_assets.why_relevant: The app-server-backed TUI changed heavily enough to affect the practical operator viewpoint. Session history, composer behavior, onboarding/auth, MCP inventory, and realtime rendering all moved recently.
analysis_implications: Do not treat the TUI as stable presentation-only glue.
When analyzing operator flows, inspect tui_app_server alongside app-server as a coupled surface.
Track deleted-in-head behavior because recent changes already included removals.
recent_signals: feat: support product-scoped plugins. (#15041)

Add device-code onboarding and ChatGPT token refresh to app-server TUI (#14952)

pilot_assets.why_relevant: This is the highest-churn boundary-contract surface. Schema changes here alter what thread/session/plugin/hook/fuzzy-search/shell-command analysis even means.
analysis_implications: Treat protocol/schema diffs as analysis-shaping inputs, not just generated noise.
Track deletions as strongly as additions because removed schema paths change the valid request/response surface.
Use this asset to drive contract-aware reporting and freshness decisions.

Simple directory mentions (#14970)

pilot_assets.why_relevant: Cheap but important structural churn signal for dependency freshness and package graph invalidation.
analysis_implications: Treat Cargo.lock movement as a freshness invalidator for cached structural artifacts.
Use it as a low-cost indicator that dependency-edge or package-version summaries may need regeneration.

pilot_assets.why_relevant: Persistence and migration changes affect thread graph interpretation, resumability, evidence, and temporal analysis.
analysis_implications: Flag migrations and thread metadata changes explicitly in summaries.
Use state as the persistence-aware companion to core when reasoning about session and agent network behavior.

pilot_assets.why_relevant: Newly emphasized execution surface. The window introduced the stub server/docs and then removed stdio transport, changing what transport assumptions are valid.
analysis_implications: Do not assume stdio transport is still a viable baseline.
Treat exec-server as an evolving transport/execution boundary worth separate reporting.
Use it as a test asset for newly introduced surfaces and deletions-in-head.
recent_signals: Remove stdio transport from exec server (#15119)

pilot_assets.why_relevant: Directly relevant to artifact-first analysis because this surface changed packaging/runtime artifact behavior.
analysis_implications: Treat artifact runtime/packaging changes as first-class inputs to metadata_baseline design.
Use artifacts as a concrete test case for output registration and artifact provenance handling.
recent_signals: feat: adapt artifacts to new packaging and 2.5.6 (#14947)

pilot_assets.why_relevant: Filesystem abstraction changes affect path semantics and therefore ownership mapping and file-based joins.
analysis_implications: Treat path and FS semantics as analysis inputs, not invisible implementation details.
Use environment as a guardrail asset when defining file-to-package ownership joins.
recent_signals: Add FS abstraction and use in view_image (#14960)

capabilities.name: Workspace discovery and normalization
capabilities.summary: Resolve the authoritative workspace root and manifest with Cargo rather than filesystem heuristics.
notes: Becomes strictly codex-rs-rooted instead of generic monorepo discovery-first.

capabilities.name: Dual Cargo metadata capture
capabilities.summary: Capture both members-only workspace metadata and resolved host-platform metadata, with feature-sensitive variants implied as optional extensions.

capabilities.name: Stable .analysis artifact layout
capabilities.summary: Emit raw, reports, and index artifacts into a canonical .analysis layout with one shared handoff anchor.
notes: Becomes a production requirement rather than a layout preference.

capabilities.name: Package inventory extraction
capabilities.summary: Derive a normalized package census from workspace metadata into a stable TSV report.
notes: Moves from generic census to active pilot input.

capabilities.name: Normalized package-edge extraction
capabilities.summary: Emit dependency edges with package-level dependency semantics including kind, optionality, default-features, requested features, and target expressions.
notes: Becomes central to the pilot rather than just part of the baseline.

capabilities.name: Target inventory extraction
capabilities.summary: Emit a normalized target inventory suitable for mapping files or crate targets back to owning packages.
notes: Becomes mandatory for ownership and path attribution.

capabilities.name: Duplicate-version detection
capabilities.summary: Capture duplicate dependency versions for explanation and package-level waste analysis.

capabilities.name: Reverse dependencies and feature causality
capabilities.summary: Support explanatory reverse dependency and feature-causality views using cargo tree outputs for interactive analysis.
notes: Defer from day-one production baseline to on-demand drilldowns.

capabilities.name: Semantic-pass capture with cheap-first execution
capabilities.summary: Run cargo check variants and persist machine-readable diagnostics before any heavier build or timings pass.
notes: Defer broad cargo check capture unless needed for one asset-focused report lane.

capabilities.name: Build-cost and hotspot observation
capabilities.summary: Persist timing output as a build-cost lens over the structural metadata graph.
notes: Push out of the first production cut.

capabilities.name: Freshness gating
capabilities.summary: Treat metadata artifacts as denied when stale and invalidate them on key execution-identity changes.
notes: Needs to incorporate Codex-specific invalidators earlier.

capabilities.name: Artifact-required downstream handoff
capabilities.summary: Require downstream package-aware and blast-radius consumers to read the baseline index or explicitly deny analysis when it is missing or stale.
notes: Becomes the immediate contract for codex-web operations.

capabilities.name: Package ownership overlay
capabilities.summary: Provide package ownership derived from manifest and target path information, capped at file/package-root precision.
notes: Becomes one of the earliest required outputs.

capabilities.name: Downstream semantic augmentation
capabilities.summary: Feed higher-level semantic skills with metadata artifacts and package overlays instead of forcing them to infer monorepo structure directly.

capabilities.name: Sidecar semantic and review overlays
capabilities.summary: Support additive sem and inspect artifacts that join back to package ownership without replacing Cargo metadata as structural truth.

capabilities.name: Queryable analysis layer
capabilities.summary: Expose query-style operations over the generated artifacts rather than only emitting raw reports.

pilot_new_signals.name: Deleted-in-head reporting
pilot_new_signals.summary: The baseline now needs an explicit way to register touched paths that no longer exist at current head.
pilot_new_signals.reason: The pilot asset set includes app-server-protocol and exec-server, which already showed deletions-in-head in the recent Codex window.

pilot_new_signals.name: Asset-bucket reporting
pilot_new_signals.summary: The baseline should summarize at the asset/package bucket level, not only per-file or per-graph-edge.
pilot_new_signals.reason: The pilot set is framed as concrete asset buckets like core, app-server, and tui_app_server.

pilot_new_signals.name: Cargo.lock freshness input
pilot_new_signals.summary: Cargo.lock movement should be treated as a first-class freshness and pilot-window signal.
pilot_new_signals.reason: Cargo.lock is itself one of the selected pilot assets and is a cheap signal of dependency graph churn.

pilot_new_signals.name: Migration-aware summaries
pilot_new_signals.summary: State and protocol-adjacent assets need summary surfaces that can mention migrations or persistence shifts.
pilot_new_signals.reason: The selected pilot assets include state and app-server-protocol, both of which evolve control/data contracts over time.

pilot_new_signals.name: Artifact-first codex-web handoff
pilot_new_signals.summary: The baseline should assume codex-web consumes normalized artifacts through just/scripts rather than direct deep integration with unstable TUI/app-server surfaces.
pilot_new_signals.reason: This matches the current decision to minimize refactor surface until upstream settles.

defer_until_surfaces_settle: reverse dependency and feature causality drilldowns
